title: Which ball bounces the highest?
description: Interactive drop-and-measure experiment for NCERT Grade 6 Science Chapter 6 'Materials Around Us', Activity 6.4. Balls of the same size but different materials are dropped from a fixed height; a physics-flavoured animation shows each bounce, a guide line marks the peak, and the bounce height is recorded into Table 6.2 with a High/Medium/Low level.

curriculum.subject: Science
curriculum.section: 6.2 How to Group Materials? (Activity 6.4: Let us explore)
concepts: The material an object is made of decides how it behaves
Bounce height as a comparable, recordable observation
Recording observations in a structured table (Table 6.2)
learningObjectives: Observe that same-sized balls bounce differently because of their material
Compare and rank bounce heights (high / medium / low)
Record experimental observations into a table
Connect a material's property (springy, hard, soft) to an outcome

designSystem.name: Singularity
conventions: import React as the literal first line
function declarations only at module scope (no arrow functions)
hooks accessed as React.useState / React.useEffect / React.useRef
no Tailwind, no lucide-react; inline SVG ball faces only
keyframes injected via useEffect into a <style> tag
ResizeObserver-driven breakpoints via a single SIZING map (S/M/L)
fixed 1fr 1fr two-column grid on M and L widths, single column only on the narrowest (S) width
always-on text captions and live numeric readouts alongside the animation

modes.experiment: Pick one ball via the material swatches, press Drop, and watch it fall, squash on impact, and bounce to its peak. The peak guide line and the table row fill in automatically.
modes.compare: Press 'Drop all together' to release all balls at the same instant in side-by-side lanes. Each ball falls and bounces with its own physics, so their differing bounce heights are visible simultaneously and the whole table fills at once.

controls: Mode tabs — 'Drop one' (single ball) and 'Drop all together' (simultaneous)
Material swatch row — in experiment mode tap to choose which ball to test; in compare mode all are active
Primary button — Drop the selected ball, or Drop all together
Reset — clears the stage and the recorded table
interactionModel.stageLayout: In compare mode the stage is split into equal vertical lanes, one per ball, each with its own ball, contact ring, dashed peak guide line (labelled in cm) and a name chip below the floor. A shared dashed line at the top marks the common drop height.

animation.impact: ball squashes; an expanding contact ring is spawned per ball
animation.rise: easeOut (decelerating) ascent to peak = bounceFactor of drop height
animation.settle: easeIn return to floor; bounce height committed to the table
animation.simultaneity: A single requestAnimationFrame loop advances every active ball each frame, so all balls in compare mode are released and animate together.

interactionModel.recording: When a drop completes, each tested ball's measured bounce (dropHeightCm * bounceFactor, rounded) is written to the table keyed by ball id, along with its High/Medium/Low level pill.

width.description: Suggested render width; layout is fully responsive via ResizeObserver.

height.description: Advisory only; component sizes itself to content.

showModeSelector.description: Show/hide the Drop & test / Compare all tabs.

animationSpeed.description: Multiplier; 2 = twice as fast, 0.5 = half speed.

additionalProps.description: Tool-specific dynamic content the agent can pass to customise the experiment.

balls.description: Override the default set of balls. Each entry fully defines a ball.

id.description: Unique id, used as table/record key.

bounceFactor.description: Fraction (0..1) of drop height the ball returns to. 0.62 = bounces to 62% of drop height.

level.description: Bounce level pill shown in the table.

fill2.description: Darker shade for the radial gradient (hex).

art.description: Which inline-SVG surface pattern to draw.

note.description: One-line explanation shown when the ball is selected.

dropHeightCm.description: The fixed height balls are dropped from, in cm. Bounce readings scale to this.

highlightBallId.description: Pre-select and emphasise this ball on load.

showMaterials.description: Show/hide the material swatch selector row.

autoRecord.description: Auto-fill the table row when a drop completes.

agentDecisionTree.Teacher wants a side-by-side comparison: Set initialMode 'compare'; the 'Drop all together' button releases every ball at once in its own lane and fills the whole table.
agentDecisionTree.Lesson is about one specific ball: Set additionalProps.highlightBallId and consider showMaterials false to reduce distraction.
agentDecisionTree.Custom set of materials (e.g. steel, cla: Pass additionalProps.balls with your own bounceFactor and level values.
agentDecisionTree.Different release height in the workshee: Set additionalProps.dropHeightCm; all bounce readings rescale.
agentDecisionTree.Want a clean animation-only view: Set showTable false and showModeSelector false.

toolCallExamples.title: Default Activity 6.4
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Drop each ball from the same height and see which one bounces back the highest.

toolCallExamples.title: Drop all balls together
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Press 'Drop all together' to release all three at once and watch which one bounces back the highest.

toolCallExamples.title: Focus on the tennis ball only
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: The springy rubber tennis ball bounces the highest. Drop it and read the bounce height.

toolCallExamples.title: Drop from 150 cm
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Now we drop from 150 cm. Notice the bounce heights are bigger but the order stays the same.

toolCallExamples.title: Custom materials — rubber vs clay vs sponge

balls.name: Rubber ball
balls.note: Rubber is very springy and returns most of the energy.

balls.name: Clay ball
balls.note: Hard, brittle clay does not spring back at all.

balls.name: Sponge ball
balls.note: Soft sponge squashes and absorbs energy.

toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Predict the order before you drop them, then test your guess.

errorHandling.empty additionalProps: Falls back to the three default textbook balls dropped from 100 cm.
errorHandling.bounceFactor out of range: Values are used as-is; keep within 0..1 for a sensible bounce.
errorHandling.unknown highlightBallId: Ignored; first ball is selected instead.
